title: The Case of the Missing Watering Hole
synopsis: When the savanna's watering hole mysteriously disappears overnight, young detective Nia teams up with worried animals to solve the puzzle and save their precious water source.

pages.text: Nia loved exploring the African savanna. Every morning, she walked through the golden grass to visit her animal friends. Today felt different. The animals were all gathered in one spot, looking very worried.

illustration.description: A young girl with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes walks through tall golden grass in the savanna. Various worried-looking animals including zebras, giraffes, and antelopes are gathered in the distance. The scene is painted in gouache with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, and golden grass swaying in the breeze.
illustration.alt: Nia walking through the savanna toward worried animals

pages.text: "What's wrong?" Nia asked Zara the zebra. Zara pointed with her nose toward the big baobab tree. "Our watering hole is gone!" she said. "It was there yesterday, but this morning there's no water at all!"

illustration.description: A zebra with expressive eyes speaks to Nia, who has long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes. Behind them is a large baobab tree, and where there should be a watering hole, there's only dry, cracked earth. The gouache painting uses bold confident strokes with warm savanna colors and textured painterly technique.
illustration.alt: Zara the zebra talking to Nia about the missing watering hole

pages.text: Nia looked at the empty spot. Yesterday it had been full of clear, cool water. Now there was only dry dirt and some strange marks in the ground. "This is a mystery," Nia said. "Don't worry, friends. I'll help you find your water!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes kneels beside the empty watering hole, examining strange marks in the dry earth. The gouache painting shows thick brushstrokes creating textured, cracked ground with mysterious tracks and marks visible in the dirt.
illustration.alt: Nia examining the empty watering hole and strange marks

pages.text: Nia got down on her hands and knees to look at the marks more closely. There were big round prints in the mud. "These look like elephant footprints," she said. Gigi the giraffe stretched her long neck down. "But why would elephants take our water?"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes crouches down examining large round elephant footprints in the mud. A tall giraffe with an expressive face leans her long neck down to look. The gouache painting uses warm savanna colors with thick brushstrokes showing detailed footprint impressions in the textured earth.
illustration.alt: Nia and Gigi examining elephant footprints in the mud

pages.text: "Let's follow the tracks," Nia suggested. The footprints led away from the empty watering hole, through the golden grass, toward the rocky hills. All the animals followed behind Nia as she carefully tracked the prints.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes leads a parade of savanna animals including zebras, antelopes, and meerkats through golden grass following a trail of footprints. Rocky hills are visible in the distance under an orange sunset sky. The gouache painting shows bold confident strokes with expressive animals and textured painterly grass.
illustration.alt: Nia leading animals following the footprint trail toward rocky hills

pages.text: As they walked, they found more clues. There were broken branches and trampled grass. "The elephants were carrying something heavy," Nia observed. She picked up a wet leaf. "And it was dripping water!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes holds a dripping wet leaf while standing near broken branches and trampled golden grass. Water droplets glisten on the leaf and ground. The gouache painting captures the wet textures with thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors in textured painterly style.
illustration.alt: Nia discovering wet leaves and broken branches as clues

pages.text: The tracks led to a hidden valley behind the rocks. Nia and her friends peeked over the edge. Below them, they saw a family of elephants. And there was their water! But it wasn't in the watering hole anymore. It was in big containers made of mud and grass.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes peers over rocky ledge with various savanna animals. Below in a hidden valley, a family of elephants stands near large mud and grass containers filled with water. The gouache painting shows the scene from above with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, and expressive elephants in textured painterly style.
illustration.alt: Nia and animals discovering elephants with water containers in hidden valley

pages.text: "Why did you take our water?" called Zara the zebra. The biggest elephant looked up. Her name was Temba, and she looked very sad. "We're sorry," Temba said. "Our baby elephant, Kesi, is sick. She needs clean water, but she's too weak to walk to the watering hole."

illustration.description: A large mother elephant named Temba with sad, expressive eyes stands protectively near a smaller, weak-looking baby elephant. Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes and other animals look on with concern. The gouache painting uses warm, sympathetic colors with thick brushstrokes showing the emotional scene in textured painterly style.
illustration.alt: Mother elephant Temba explaining about her sick baby Kesi

pages.text: Nia's warm kind eyes filled with understanding. "Oh no! We want to help baby Kesi get better," she said. "But all the other animals need water too. There has to be a way we can share and help everyone!"

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes speaks compassionately while surrounded by both the elephant family and the other savanna animals. Everyone looks hopeful as they listen to her words. The gouache painting shows unity and cooperation with expressive animals rendered in thick brushstrokes and warm savanna colors.
illustration.alt: Nia bringing elephants and other animals together with compassion

pages.text: Nia had an idea. "What if we move the water containers closer to the watering hole? Then baby Kesi can drink without walking far, and we can refill the big watering hole for everyone else!" All the animals thought this was a wonderful plan.

illustration.description: Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes gestures excitedly as she explains her plan to a mixed group of elephants, zebras, giraffes, and other savanna animals. Everyone looks excited and hopeful. The gouache painting shows animated expressions with thick brushstrokes, warm colors, and textured painterly technique creating an atmosphere of cooperation.
illustration.alt: Nia explaining her plan to help everyone share the water

pages.text: Everyone worked together. The strong elephants carefully moved the water containers. The tall giraffes used their long necks to pour water back into the watering hole. Even the small meerkats helped by carrying wet leaves to baby Kesi to keep her cool.

illustration.description: A bustling scene of cooperation showing Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes coordinating as elephants move containers, giraffes pour water with their long necks, and meerkats carry wet leaves. The gouache painting captures the teamwork with bold confident strokes, expressive animals, and warm savanna colors in golden grass under an orange sunset.
illustration.alt: All animals working together to solve the water problem

pages.text: Soon, the watering hole was full again, and baby Kesi had her own special drinking spot nearby. All the animals could drink whenever they needed to. "Thank you for solving our mystery, Nia!" said Temba. "And thank you for helping us work together," added Zara. Nia smiled. Another mystery solved with kindness and teamwork!

illustration.description: A celebratory scene showing Nia with long braids with colorful beads, dark brown skin, warm kind eyes surrounded by happy animals at the restored watering hole. Baby elephant Kesi drinks from her special container while other animals drink from the main watering hole. The gouache painting shows joy and satisfaction with thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, golden grass, and an orange sunset creating a perfect happy ending in textured painterly style.
illustration.alt: Happy ending with all animals sharing water and baby Kesi healthy

metadata.illustrationStyle: gouache painting, thick brushstrokes, warm savanna colors, expressive animals, textured painterly, golden grass, orange sunset, bold confident strokes

questions.question: What was missing from the watering hole?
questions.explanation: The water from the watering hole had disappeared overnight, which started the mystery.

questions.question: What kind of footprints did Nia find?
questions.explanation: Nia found large, round elephant footprints in the mud around the empty watering hole.

questions.question: Why did the elephants take the water?
questions.explanation: The elephants took the water because baby Kesi was too sick and weak to walk to the watering hole.

questions.question: How did Nia solve the problem?
questions.explanation: Nia's solution involved all the animals working together to share the water and help baby Kesi.

questions.question: What helped the animals solve their problem?
questions.explanation: The animals solved their water problem through kindness, understanding, and working together as a team.
